criterion: The Monitor UI renders live host vitals: CPU load and memory usage.
observed: Vitals grid highlighted: CPU card reads 14% with a usage bar and 'load 2.99 · 2.40 · 1.78 · 16 cores'; Memory card reads 19% with a bar and '12.0 GB / 62.8 GB used'. Real host counters via systeminformation.

criterion: Per-mount disk usage with the warn/crit thresholds (85% warn / 95% crit styling) - the disk section.
observed: Disks section highlighted, one row per real mount with used/total, a usage bar and percentage: / 57.2 GB/435 GB · 13%, /sys/firmware/efi/efivars · 13%, /boot 72.5 MB/880 MB · 9%, /boot/efi 6.1 MB/104 MB · 6%. Every mount is below the 85% warn threshold, so all bars render in the ok/default severity; the warn/crit styling (diskSeverity 85/95) is implemented but nothing currently crosses it - filmed honestly.
Network card highlighted showing RX 234 KB/s and TX 512 KB/s - real per-second throughput from si.networkStats aggregated over 3 interfaces.

criterion: garrison-* systemd user units section (Linux) renders; empty state acceptable if none running.
observed: Garrison units section highlighted showing the honest empty state 'no garrison-* systemd user units'. `systemctl --user list-units garrison-*` returns none on this host, so the Linux-only listing renders its empty state (captioned 'none active').

criterion: Vitals REFRESH live across the 5s client cadence - a changed value proves it is not a static snapshot.
assert: .vitals-grid contains "CPU" (highlighted); two frames extracted 9.2s apart inside the 9s hold (frames/ac5-live-early.png t=22.2 vs frames/ac5-live-late.png t=31.4) compared for changed values
observed: Across the ~9s hold (which spans the 5s refresh cadence) multiple live values changed: CPU 19% to 15%, load1 3.42 to 3.66, Memory used 12.6 GB to 12.5 GB, Network RX 12.2 to 8.5 KB/s, TX 47.8 to 50.4 KB/s. Network RX also moved across earlier beats (18.8 to 234 to 18.3 KB/s). Confirms the SSE-driven vitals refresh live, not a frozen snapshot.

criterion: Strictly display-only: no control/action buttons anywhere on the vitals surface.
assert: section.vitals contains "CPU" (whole surface highlighted); off-camera runtime DOM count of interactive elements inside section.vitals
observed: The entire vitals surface (CPU / Memory / Network / Disks / Garrison units - 5 cards) is highlighted with no buttons or controls visible. Runtime DOM check of section.vitals: 0 <button>, 0 <a>, 0 <input>/<select>/<textarea>, 0 [role=button], 0 [onclick]. Display-only confirmed.
